reasoning:
The question asks whether Fabiano Caruana's Elo rating on three resolution dates (2025-11-02, 2025-11-25, 2026-01-24) was >= 1% higher than his rating on 2025-10-26 (which was 2789.0). A 1% increase requires an Elo of approximately 2816.89 (i.e., at least 2817). 
FIDE publishes classical ratings once a month (usually on the 1st). The resolution dates fall in November 2025, November 2025, and January 2026, meaning they check the November 1, 2025 and January 1, 2026 rating lists.
Background searches showed that in December 2025, Caruana was inactive and his rating was below 2800 (only Carlsen and Nakamura were >= 2800 as per chessbase). In January 2026, chessbase reported "no change in Elo ratings" for top players such as him, and he remained at #3 behind Nakamura. His April 2026 rating on Wikipedia is 2793. 
Therefore, on all three resolution dates, his rating was well below 2817. I am very confident the answer is NO for all dates, with a tiny probability margin to account for extremely unexpected edge cases like retroactive rating corrections or FIDE publishing anomalies.
